payload.key_insight: BAC's strong 4Q25 results across Global Markets (sales and trading up 10%, equities up 23%) and investment banking — combined with CEO bullishness on 2026 U.S. economic growth — suggest a favorable operating environment for GS, which has significantly higher revenue concentration in trading and investment banking than BAC. The pickup in equities trading and resilient IB fee activity are particularly strong leading indicators for GS estimate revisions given GS's outsized exposure to those lines.
specific_quotes: Sales and trading revenue of $4.5 billion, up 10%, including and excluding net debit valuation adjustment (DVA) losses of $17 million. 15th consecutive quarter of year-over-year growth — Equities revenue up 23% to $2 billion, including and excluding net DVA

With consumers and businesses proving resilient, as well as the regulatory environment and tax and trade policies coming into sharper focus, we expect further economic growth in the year ahead. While any number of risks continue, we are bullish on the U.S. economy in 2026.
Total Corporation investment banking fees (excl. self-led) of $1.7 billion, up 1% — #3 investment banking fee ranking for 2025
